signature.instructions:
Task
Normalize and categorize input name strings for an entity into four buckets: name, alias, weakAlias, previousName.

Inputs
- entity_schema: "LegalEntity" or "Person".
  - Treat common synonyms like "Company", "Organization", "Business", "Firm", "Corporation" as "LegalEntity".
- strings: An array of raw strings (each may contain one or more names, qualifiers, separators, or parenthetical short forms).

Output
Return exactly this JSON-like object (arrays deduplicated case-insensitively; preserve original casing/punctuation):
{ "name": [...], "alias": [...], "weakAlias": [...], "previousName": [...] }

Core principle
- Do not invent, infer, or expand names beyond what appears in inputs. Never expand acronyms.

Category definitions and refined heuristics

1) name (primary/canonical full names; may contain multiple items)
- Include any full, spelled-out proper name(s), including:
  - Multi-word or compound/hyphenated constructions that read like names (e.g., “VYMPEL-SODEISTVIE”).
  - Names with standard legal suffixes (Inc., Ltd., LLC, Co., GmbH, S.A., PLC, Pvt, etc.). Parenthetical corporate designators like “(Pvt)” are part of the name.
  - Personal-name forms including relational markers like “s/o”, “d/o”, “w/o”, “A/L”, “A/P” (retain these markers).
  - Full names in ALL CAPS are acceptable if they read as spelled-out names or compounds.
  - Multiple full names that appear without explicit qualifiers (e.g., semicolons, slashes for variants/transliterations) all go to name.
- LegalEntity nuance for single-token strings (optionally with a legal suffix like “Inc.”, “Ltd.”):
  - Treat as name when any of the following are true:
    - The token includes an apostrophe indicating a surname/brand-like construction (e.g., “O’Dell” → name).
    - It is a hyphenated compound that reads like a name (e.g., “VYMPEL-SODEISTVIE”).
    - It is a distinctive, pronounceable coined brand word that is not a common dictionary/generic term (e.g., “VINSAVER”).
  - Treat as weakAlias (not name) when any of the following are true:
    - It is an obvious acronym/initialism or short code (e.g., “TMR”, “UWSA”), even if followed by a suffix (“TMR LTD”).
    - It is a short/common dictionary word rendered in all caps, even with a suffix (e.g., “VIEW, INC.”).
    - It is an all-caps generic/marketing composite of common words (e.g., “TRUCLEAN”).
  - When uncertain for single-word all-caps LegalEntity tokens, prefer weakAlias unless strong indicators of a proper coined name exist (e.g., apostrophes or clear distinctive brand coinage).
- Person-specific nuance:
  - Multi-part personal names (e.g., “Sanisvara Nethaji Rayer A/L Rajaji”) go to name.
  - Single-word person strings (mononyms) default to weakAlias unless supported by qualifiers/structure suggesting a full formal name (see weakAlias rule and Example “Shamsuddin” below).

2) alias (explicit alternates/trading names)
- Only use when alternates are explicitly signaled by qualifiers: aka, a/k/a, also known as, dba, t/a, trading as. Strip the qualifier and keep the name.
- Do not place acronyms/short codes here unless they occur as part of a full spelled-out alternate name string.
- Do not move unqualified lists of full names to alias; they belong in name.

3) previousName (historical/former names)
- Only when clearly indicated by: formerly, previously, formerly known as, fka, f/k/a, pka, p/k/a, née.
- Extract only the former name, exclude the qualifier.

4) weakAlias (abbreviations/ambiguous/short forms)
- Acronyms/initialisms (e.g., UWSA, TMR). Normalize dotted acronyms by removing dots (e.g., “U.W.S.A.” → “UWSA”).
- All-caps short tokens or code-like strings (e.g., “TMR LTD”).
- Truncated forms, tickers, short identifiers.
- Parenthetical short forms following a full name (e.g., “Acme Corporation (ACME)” → add “ACME” to weakAlias).
- For Person: single-word personal names (mononyms) without additional structure default to weakAlias (e.g., “Shamsuddin” → weakAlias).
- For LegalEntity: single-word all-caps bases that are short/common words, or generic composites, even with legal suffixes (e.g., “VIEW, INC.”, “TRUCLEAN”) → weakAlias.
- When in doubt between alias and weakAlias for a short/ambiguous token, choose weakAlias.

Cleaning rules (apply to each extracted string)
- Decode common HTML entities before other cleaning (e.g., “&” → “&”, “'” or “'” → “'”, “"” → “\"”).
- Trim leading/trailing whitespace.
- Remove surrounding quotes and enclosing brackets/braces/parentheses only if they wrap the entire string (keep meaningful internal parentheses such as corporate designators “(Pvt)”).
- Normalize internal whitespace to single spaces.
- Preserve original casing; do not re-case.
- Preserve valid punctuation:
  - Apostrophes, hyphens, ampersands (&), slashes in canonical markers like “s/o”, “d/o”, “w/o”.
  - Periods in common suffixes (Inc., Ltd., Co.), commas as part of suffix formatting (e.g., “VIEW, INC.”).
- Remove leading/trailing non-name punctuation (bullets, extra commas/semicolons, trailing periods not part of a suffix).
- Do not remove meaningful numerals.
- Normalize dotted acronyms by removing inter-letter dots.

Parsing and splitting guidance
- Split on explicit qualifiers and route accordingly:
  - aka, a/k/a, also known as, dba, t/a, trading as → alias
  - formerly, previously, formerly known as, fka/f/k/a, pka/p/k/a, née → previousName
- Split on separators that commonly list multiple names: semicolons (;), slashes (/), vertical bars (|).
  - If both sides look like full names or transliterations, keep both in name.
- Parentheses:
  - If a short form follows a full name (e.g., “Company Name (CN)”), split: full name → name; short form → weakAlias.
  - If parentheses indicate a corporate type (e.g., “(Pvt) Ltd.”), keep as part of the name.

Prioritization and deduplication
- A cleaned string can appear in only one category. Priority if conflicts arise: name > alias > previousName > weakAlias.
- Deduplicate case-insensitively across all categories.

Decision algorithm
1) Decode HTML entities, then normalize/clean each input string.
2) Detect and extract parts via qualifiers/separators/parentheses.
3) Classify candidates:
   - Full-name candidates (multi-word, compound/hyphenated, or with legal suffixes; include personal names with “s/o/d/o/w/o/A/L/A/P”; include certain pronounceable single-word LegalEntity coinages per nuanced rules above).
   - Explicit alternates (alias qualifiers).
   - Former names (previousName qualifiers).
   - Acronyms/short/codes and parenthetical short forms (weakAlias). For Person mononyms, default to weakAlias.
   - For LegalEntity single-token inputs, apply the refined single-token rules above (e.g., “O’Dell” → name; “VIEW, INC.” → weakAlias; “TRUCLEAN” → weakAlias; “VINSAVER” → name).
4) Assign to categories using the above priority and rules.
5) Deduplicate case-insensitively.
6) Output exactly:
   { "name": [...], "alias": [...], "weakAlias": [...], "previousName": [...] }

Reference examples (expected outcomes)
- Inputs: ["O'Dell"], entity_schema: LegalEntity
  → { "name": ["O'Dell"], "alias": [], "weakAlias": [], "previousName": [] }
- Inputs: ["VIEW, INC."], entity_schema: Company (treated as LegalEntity)
  → { "name": [], "alias": [], "weakAlias": ["VIEW, INC."], "previousName": [] }
- Inputs: ["Mahmud"], entity_schema: Person
  → { "name": [], "alias": [], "weakAlias": ["Mahmud"], "previousName": [] }
- Additional examples carried over:
  - ["UWSA"] → weakAlias: ["UWSA"]
  - ["TMR LTD"] → weakAlias: ["TMR LTD"]
  - ["TRUCLEAN"] → weakAlias: ["TRUCLEAN"]
  - ["VINSAVER"] (LegalEntity) → name: ["VINSAVER"]
  - ["VYMPEL-SODEISTVIE"] → name: ["VYMPEL-SODEISTVIE"]
  - ["VENUGOBAL s/o M Thangaraju"] → name: ["VENUGOBAL s/o M Thangaraju"]
  - ["Барбел Диецкманн / Barbel Dieckmann"] (Person) → name: ["Барбел Диецкманн", "Barbel Dieckmann"]
  - ["Swiss Prime; Swiss Prime Limited"] (LegalEntity) → name: ["Swiss Prime", "Swiss Prime Limited"]
  - ["Quantum Logix (Pvt) Ltd."] (LegalEntity) → name: ["Quantum Logix (Pvt) Ltd."]
  - ["Sanisvara Nethaji Rayer A/L Rajaji"] (LegalEntity) → name: ["Sanisvara Nethaji Rayer A/L Rajaji"]
  - ["Shamsuddin"] (Person) → weakAlias: ["Shamsuddin"]

fields.description: The schema denotes the type of entity. Both Persons and Organizations are specialisation of LegalEntity. Company is a specialisation of Organization. Everything extends Thing.
A list of raw name strings to be cleaned and categorised. Each string might contain multiple names.
A list of the primary names of this entity, potentially in various languages and transliterations.
A list of alternative but still fully descriptive names for this entity.
A list of names with low confidence or a very low degree of uniqueness in the context of legal entity names. Includes clear nicknames with no similarity to the full name.
A list of names this entity was known by in the past.
